However the company “Anthropic” has a terrible support team who does not respond to requests at all, it’s to the point it’s completely embarrassing and they should be ashamed to call themselves a support team when there is no support to be seen. 

My account was disabled for being a “child” when really I’m 19. I received an automated email saying to appeal using Yoti for face verification as the method for appeal. I did that and all it did was redirect me from Yoti which reminder it went through on Yoti’s end picking me up as a 19 year old adult. But then it redirects me to Claude with my account still disabled. 

So I contacted support by filling out their appeal form both on their google forms appeal page and their official website. I have a month before appeal is no longer available and it’s been almost 4 weeks and to my surprise no response.

Pathetic and absolutely disgusting team of “experts” who are experts at letting peo

reviews.content: Be careful, you can pay and get banned without explanation. Support won't answer any questions. Refunds are impossible. The only thing the cloud managed to ask was what time it was.

reviews.title: Loved It With Premium… Until They Banned Me After 1 Week
reviews.content:
I got Claude Premium because the free version was good and I wanted faster replies and extra features. For one full week it was honestly great — really helpful with school assignments, coding practice, and creative writing. I was impressed and thought it was worth the money.
Then, right in the middle of a normal conversation, it suddenly banned my account saying I’m not 18. I’m 16. I never lied about my age or asked for anything weird — just regular chats. No warning, no appeal process, no way to verify age. Just gone. After I already paid.
Super frustrating. Went from “this is my new favorite AI” to deleted app in seconds. Feels like they don’t want anyone under 18 using it at all, even for normal stuff.
Premium is nice while it lasts… but one week? Not worth it.
Don’t waste your money if you’re under 18. Delete it and download Grok instead — way more fun, actually helpful, no random bans, and the voice + image stuff is awesome. Best AI app hands down.
Huge disappointment.

reviews.title: Ridiculously limited free version.
reviews.content:
No search feature unless you pay…. That is just obscene and absurd.

Like all AI, it will flip-flop …. so make sure you use critical thinking and double check everything it tells you.

Also has very limited voice options.

I’ve used ChatGPT, Perplexity, Gemini, and Claude. Claude ranked third to the last. I don’t see what the hype is about (except it’s got a GenZ/millennial vibe to it.)

But the lack of search feature keeps it too limited  for me…..so if you’re just using it for free stuff like I am, I would recommend ChatGPT for talk, and Gemini for anything you need to upload unlimited photos for, and Perplexity for technical troubleshooting. I can’t recommend Claude for anything. Skip Claude altogether is my recommendation. 
Unless you’re looking to pay for it … then go nuts ;)

reviews.title: Selective Censorship and Linguistic Bias: An Ecosystem Stuck in the Past.
reviews.content:
"It is unacceptable that in 2026 despite all the processing power we boast, the ecosystem collapses when faced with the natural use of the Spanish language. I have repeatedly confirmed that the system applies automatic censorship to words fully accepted by the RAE (Royal Spanish Academy), treating them as offenses when they are merely part of a raw description of reality.
This 'fragile' sensitivity not only breaks the flow of deep analytical conversation but feels like a form of linguistic racism. By punishing original terms of our language under a foreign standard of political correctness, you are limiting the user's ability to express themselves honestly.
You can censor the words, but you cannot change the reality of the facts being communicated. If the goal is to create a useful tool for visionaries and analytical minds, stop muzzling the Spanish language and focus on making sure the system can handle a direct truth without breaking."

reviews.title: Why tung Sahur is the goat.
reviews.content: In the rapidly evolving landscape of digital folklore and "brainrot" memes, one figure has emerged as an undisputed titan of absurdity and cultural fusion: Tung Tung Tung Sahur. Emerging in early 2025, this AI-generated, anthropomorphic log wielding a baseball bat has transcended the typical lifespan of a viral meme to become a cultural cornerstone, earning the title "GOAT" (Greatest of All Time) through its unique mix of cultural reverence, chaotic absurdity, and unparalleled memetic power. At its core, Tung Tung Tung Sahur is a brilliant parody of a heartfelt tradition. The name itself is an onomatopoeia for the kentongan, a traditional wooden slit drum used in Indonesia to wake neighborhoods for the early morning sahur meal during Ramadan. By taking a sacred communal activity and transforming it into a 7-foot, 300lb, wide-eyed, bat-wielding wooden anomaly, creators have blended deep-rooted cultural awareness with postmodern humor. This juxtaposition makes the content both nostalgic
